01_initial_request.input_data: ChIP-seq, DNase-seq, and whole genome bisulfite sequencing (WGBS) data
01_initial_request.user_intent: To develop a computational tool to predict the effect of DNA methylation on transcription factor binding strength in every position within a transcription factor's motif using in vivo data
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Predictions of the magnitude and direction of change to transcription factor binding strength for every position within a transcription factor's motif

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SEMpl is a previously published expectation-maximization method for predicting transcription factor binding affinity. [Gap] Need to generate kmer sequences from position weight matrices for analysis. [Decision] To achieve motif-based kmer generation, chose to use SEMpl to perform kmer generation from PWM on transcription factor motifs, expecting to obtain kmers with PWM scores under the TFM-P VALUE threshold.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: All kmers with PWM score under the TFM-P VALUE threshold are generated for the given transcription factor

references.relevance: Original SEMpl method publication describing the expectation-maximization approach

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SEMpl has generated kmers from PWM. [Gap] Need to simulate all possible sequence variants to assess binding effects. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive variant analysis, chose to use SEMpl to perform in silico variant generation on each position of transcription factor motifs, expecting to obtain SNP kmer lists with all possible base changes.

parameters.operation: Generate all possible in silico variants along each kmer

02_agent_trajectory.observation: All possible in silico variants generated for each position of transcription factor motifs

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SEMpl has generated variant kmers. [Gap] Need to map kmers to genomic locations in open chromatin regions. [Decision] To achieve genome-wide binding assessment, chose to use SEMpl to perform kmer alignment on DNase-seq open chromatin regions, expecting to obtain aligned kmer loci with shared nucleotide and motif positions.

parameters.input: DNase-seq data for open chromatin regions
parameters.operation: Align kmers to genome in regions of open chromatin
parameters.grouping: Shared nucleotide and motif position (e.g., C in position 3 of motif)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Kmers aligned to genome in open chromatin regions with analogous ChIP-seq scores averaged for each kmer locus

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SEMpl has aligned kmers to genome. [Gap] Need to calculate binding affinity predictions from ChIP-seq signal. [Decision] To achieve binding affinity estimation, chose to use SEMpl to perform ChIP-seq signal averaging on aligned kmer loci, expecting to obtain matrix of predictions for each of four nucleotides at each motif position.

parameters.operation: Average ChIP-seq scores for each kmer locus
parameters.output: Matrix of predictions for 4 nucleotides at each motif position

02_agent_trajectory.observation: SEMpl outputs matrix of predictions for each of the four nucleotides at each position of transcription factor motif

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SEMpl has generated base predictions without methylation. [Gap] Need to incorporate DNA methylation effects on binding affinity. [Decision] To achieve methylation-aware predictions, chose to use SEMplMe to perform WGBS data integration on C and G SNPs at each motif position, expecting to obtain methylation proportion calculations using weighted sum equation.

parameters.equation: Σ(Mk * Sk)/n where M=methylation proportion, S=ChIP-seq signal, n=total kmers
parameters.requirement: Matched cell types for ChIP-seq, DNase-seq, and WGBS data

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cytosines divided into methylated and non-methylated components for each position within motif

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SEMplMe has calculated methylation proportions. [Gap] Need to generate final visualization matrix including methylated nucleotides. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive binding prediction output, chose to use SEMplMe to perform 6-nucleotide SNP effect matrix generation on all motif positions, expecting to obtain matrix including A, C, G, T, M (methylated C), and W (G opposite methylated C).

parameters.nucleotides: 6 nucleotides (A, C, G, T, M, W)
parameters.normalization: log2 normalized to endogenous binding baseline set to 0
parameters.output_files: .me.sem matrix, .semplot.me.pdf visualization, .sterr standard error, .me.totals ChIP-seq signal
parameters.quality_control: -log10(P-value) threshold of 3.15 from 100 t-tests of 1000 randomly chosen kmers

02_agent_trajectory.observation: SEMplMe output displayed as all 6 nucleotides at every position along motif, with values as log2 normalized to baseline

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SEMplMe has generated methylation-aware predictions. [Gap] Need to validate predictions against independent in vitro binding data. [Decision] To achieve external validation, chose to use Protein Binding Microarray (PBM) data comparison to perform correlation analysis on SEMplMe predictions, expecting to obtain agreement metrics between predictions and experimental binding affinity.

parameters.transcription_factors: 8 transcription factors (CEBPA, CEBPB, CEBPD, CREB1, ATF4, JUN, JUND, CEBPG)
parameters.metric: Microarray Z-scores for methylated and unmethylated binding sites

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Modest agreement observed between SEMplMe predictions and PBM data (R²=0.67); reduced agreement without methylation (R²=0.56)

references.citation: Mann IK, et al. Genome Res. 2013;23:988–97 [9]
references.relevance: Previously published PBM data used for validation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PBM validation showed modest agreement. [Gap] Need additional functional validation using quantitative binding assays. [Decision] To achieve functional validation, chose to use Electrophoretic Mobility Shift Assay (EMSA) data comparison to perform dissociation constant analysis on SEMplMe predictions, expecting to obtain correlation between predictions and in vitro binding measurements.

parameters.measurement: Kd values from densitometric scanning by ImageJ and Excel Solver Package
parameters.conditions: Methylated, hemi-methylated, and unmethylated binding sites

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Marginal agreement with predictions (R²=0.65); ATF4 R²=0.92, CEBPB R²=0.17 due to limited methylated site availability

references.citation: Mann IK, et al. Genome Res. 2013;23:988–97 [9]
references.relevance: Previously published EMSA data for ATF4 and CEBPB

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SEMplMe predictions validated against PBM and EMSA. [Gap] Need to compare against existing methylation-sensitive TF prediction methods. [Decision] To achieve method comparison, chose to use Methyl-Spec-seq comparison to perform CTCF matrix correlation analysis on SEMplMe predictions, expecting to obtain performance metrics relative to SELEX-based predictions.

parameters.kmer_set: Associated with active CTCF binding, includes methylated and unmethylated sequences

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Modest agreement between matrices (R²=0.56); SEMplMe outperforms Methyl-Spec-seq for ChIP-seq correlation (SEMplMe R²=0.25 vs Methyl-Spec-seq R²=0.04)

references.citation: Zuo Z, et al. Sci Adv. 2017 [12]
references.relevance: Methyl-Spec-seq method using in vitro SELEX for methylation effect prediction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Methyl-Spec-seq comparison completed. [Gap] Need to validate against methylation occupancy data from microarray. [Decision] To achieve methylation occupancy validation, chose to use Illumina 450k microarray analysis to perform inverse correlation analysis on SEMplMe predictions, expecting to obtain correlation between predicted binding disruption and observed methylation levels.

parameters.probe_mapping: Microarray probes mapped to genome using JASPAR CORE 2022 predicted TF binding site database
parameters.sequence_length: 61 bp sequences encompassing predicted CTCF binding sites
parameters.scoring_matrices: CTCF specific matrices from SEMplMe, SEMpl, and Methyl-Spec-seq
parameters.statistical_test: One tailed-tests with 0.95 CI using cocor R package

02_agent_trajectory.observation: SEMplMe shows more robust inverse correlation with methylation occupancy (SEMplMe R²=0.176, SEMpl R²=0.04, Methyl-Spec-seq R²=0.096)

references.citation: Reinius LE, et al. PLoS ONE. 2012;7:e41361 [21]
Castro-Mondragon JA, et al. Nucleic Acids Res. 2022;50:D165–73 [22]
references.relevance: JASPAR CORE 2022 database for TF binding site prediction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SEMplMe validated against multiple datasets. [Gap] Need to assess cell type specificity of methylation effects. [Decision] To achieve cell type specificity analysis, chose to use correlation analysis to perform cross-cell type comparison on SEMplMe outputs, expecting to obtain correlation coefficients between different cell lines for methylated sites.

parameters.comparison: SEMplMe outputs for methylated sites (MW) between cell types

02_agent_trajectory.observation: High correlation between H1-hESC and K562 (R²=0.91), reduced correlation to HepG2 (R²=0.43); p-values <=0.01 for HepG2 comparisons

references.citation: Xuan Lin QX, et al. Nucleic Acids Res. 2019;47:D145–54 [15]
references.relevance: MethMotif data supporting cell type specific methylation patterns

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Multi-method validation including PBM data comparison, EMSA data comparison, Methyl-Spec-seq comparison, Illumina 450k microarray analysis, and cell type specificity correlation analysis

PBM_correlation.interpretation: Modest agreement between SEMplMe predictions and PBM Z-scores for 8 transcription factors across 16 DNA sequences

PBM_without_methylation.interpretation: Reduced correlation when using SEMpl scores without methylation, demonstrating methylation data improves predictions

EMSA_correlation.interpretation: Marginal agreement with EMSA data for ATF4 and CEBPB; ATF4 R²=0.92, CEBPB R²=0.17

CTCF_ChipSeq_correlation.interpretation: SEMplMe outperforms Methyl-Spec-seq (R²=0.04) when comparing scores to CTCF ChIP-seq signal

Illumina_450k_correlation.interpretation: SEMplMe shows more robust inverse correlation with methylation occupancy compared to SEMpl (R²=0.04) and Methyl-Spec-seq (R²=0.096)

cell_type_correlation_H1_K562.interpretation: High correlation of SEMplMe outputs for methylated JUN sites between H1-hESC and K562 cell lines

cell_type_correlation_HepG2.interpretation: Reduced correlation to HepG2, suggesting methylation drives cell type specificity for JUN

quality_control_threshold.interpretation: Threshold set to report confidence in SEM plot from 100 t-tests of 1000 randomly chosen kmers

sensitive_vs_insensitive_pvalue.interpretation: Significant difference in methylated SEM scores between previously classified sensitive (62% sensitive loci) and insensitive (85% insensitive loci) transcription factors

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. SEMplMe successfully predicts the magnitude and direction of change to transcription factor binding strength for every position within a transcription factor's motif using in vivo data (ChIP-seq, DNase-seq, WGBS). The tool validates known methylation sensitive and insensitive positions, identifies cell type specific binding driven by methylation, and outperforms SELEX-based predictions (Methyl-Spec-seq) for CTCF. Predictions agree with in vitro experimental methods (PBM R²=0.67, EMSA R²=0.65) and show improved correlation with in vivo binding data compared to methods without methylation integration.
